name: Copper
mcp_notes: While one search result mentions an 'MCP server' in the context of Merge's integration, there is no evidence of an official Copper-authored MCP server.

evidence.claim: The Copper Developer API provides a RESTful interface for accessing Copper resources.
To register an application for OAuth2.0, developers must contact Copper to receive client_id and client_secret credentials.
Access tokens are provided as Bearer tokens in the Authorization header.
Search citation: Copper API Integration - Build & Ship Faster with Merge

api_notes: The API provides a RESTful interface to access most Copper CRM resources. Note: There is significant ambiguity in the search results between the Copper CRM (copper.com) and a digital asset management platform (copper.co) sharing a similar name. The provided documentation at developer.copper.com specifically refers to the CRM API.
one_liner: Copper is a CRM platform that provides a REST API for developers to build applications and integrate with its resources.
blocker: The developer must contact Copper to register an application and obtain client credentials.
access_notes: To register an application for the OAuth2.0 flow, developers must contact Copper to provide the application name, purpose, and a secure callback URL.
auth_notes: OAuth2.0 is the recommended method. The API also supports legacy API keys, though OAuth2.0 is preferred for third-party integrations. Bearer tokens are used in the Authorization header after completing the OAuth2.0 flow.
